description: Comprehensive modernization of MeOS from Win32/GDI + MSBuild + MySQL to CMake + React/TypeScript + SQLite, producing a cross-platform single-binary application

userStories.title: Fix include case sensitivity
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all #include directives to match the actual filename casing on disk so that the code compiles on case-sensitive filesystems (Linux).
acceptanceCriteria: Every #include "..." directive uses exactly the same casing as the file on disk
A verification script confirms zero case mismatches
userStories.notes: 147 include mismatches fixed in code/ directory. Verification script verify_includes.py added.

userStories.title: Extract utility functions from gdioutput
userStories.description: As a developer, I want widen(), narrow(), toUTF8(), fromUTF8() moved out of gdioutput to meos_util.h/cpp so that domain files don't depend on the GUI header.
acceptanceCriteria: widen(), narrow(), toUTF8(), fromUTF8() are available from meos_util.h or a new string_util.h
Domain files that only needed these functions no longer include gdioutput.h
gdioutput retains thin wrappers or using declarations for backward compatibility
userStories.notes: widen(), narrow(), toUTF8(), fromUTF8(), recodeToWide(), recodeToNarrow() moved to meos_util.h/cpp. gdioutput methods updated as thin wrappers. 1100+ calls replaced globally. gdioutput.h include removed from machinecontainer.cpp and infoserver.cpp.

userStories.title: Replace Win32-specific string functions in domain code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Windows-only string functions replaced with standard C++ equivalents in domain files so that domain code compiles on any platform.
acceptanceCriteria: No direct calls to _wtoi, sprintf_s, swprintf_s, _itow_s in domain .cpp/.h files
Replacements use standard C++ (<cstdlib>, <string>, <cwchar>) or cross-platform wrappers
userStories.notes: Replaced Win32-specific string functions (_wtoi, _itow_s, swprintf_s, sprintf_s, _stricmp, _wcsicmp, lstrcmpi, _wtof, _wtoi64) with standard C++ or portable equivalents in domain code. Added compareStringIgnoreCase wrappers to meos_util.h/cpp.

userStories.title: Replace Win32 types with standard types in domain code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Win32-specific type aliases (DWORD, BOOL) replaced with standard C++ types in domain code so that no Windows headers are needed.
acceptanceCriteria: No DWORD or Win32 BOOL usage in domain .cpp/.h files
userStories.notes: Replaced DWORD, BOOL, WORD, BYTE, LPWSTR with standard types in domain files (o*.cpp/h, generalresult.*, metalist.*, datadefiners.h, xmlparser.*, meos_util.*, onlineinput.h, MeosSQL.*). Added #include <cstdint> to headers as required.

userStories.title: Normalize path separators in domain code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hardcoded backslash path separators replaced with cross-platform alternatives in domain code.
acceptanceCriteria: Domain files use std::filesystem::path or portable path construction
Hardcoded backslash in file paths replaced (non-escape-sequence uses)
userStories.notes: Normalized hardcoded backslashes in path literals and construction across domain files. Replaced Win32-specific _wsplitpath_s and GetFileAttributes with std::filesystem::path and std::filesystem::exists in oEvent.cpp, meos.cpp, image.cpp, HTMLWriter.cpp, and meos_util.cpp. Fixed hardcoded backslash in TabList.cpp.

userStories.title: Decouple oEvent from Tab* UI classes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want oEvent.cpp decoupled from Tab UI classes (TabList, TabAuto, TabSI) so that the domain aggregate root has no knowledge of the UI layer.
acceptanceCriteria: oEvent.cpp no longer includes TabList.h, TabAuto.h, or TabSI.h
TabList::baseButtons() call replaced with a callback or interface
TabAuto::tabAutoKillMachines() call replaced with a callback or interface
TabSI::getSI().setSubSecondMode() call replaced with a callback or interface
Tab classes register their callbacks during application initialization

userStories.title: CMake build system
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to build MeOS with CMake so that I can compile on any platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Top-level CMakeLists.txt exists and builds a minimal executable
vcpkg is integrated as the dependency manager via toolchain file
vcpkg.json manifest declares all external dependencies
Precompiled headers are configured for build performance

userStories.title: Modular source layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I want source code organized in logical modules so that the codebase is navigable and each layer has clear boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ directory created with subdirectories: app/, domain/, net/, db/, util/, io/
CMake targets defined per module (static libraries + main executable)
Module dependencies are explicit in CMake (no circular dependencies)
Header include paths configured so bare #include "oBase.h" still works within modules
Builds and links successfully with the new layout

userStories.title: Test infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a proper test framework so that I can write and run tests for all modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Test targets in CMake for each module (ctest runs all tests)
At least one smoke test that compiles and runs
Vitest configured for React frontend with React Testing Library

userStories.title: Core utils migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want fundamental utility functions and exception handling migrated to src/util/ so they are available to all modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Wide/narrow string conversion (string2Wide, wide2String, narrow, widen) works
Win32 function replacements (_itow_s -> to_wstring, sprintf_s -> snprintf)
Unit tests for string conversion and time formatting

userStories.title: Parsers migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want XML and CSV parsing utilities migrated to src/util/.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit tests for xmlparser and csvparser

userStories.title: Time handling migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want time-related utilities migrated to src/util/.

userStories.title: Localization migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the i18n/localization system migrated to src/util/ with cross-platform file loading.
acceptanceCriteria: Win32 resource loading replaced with std::ifstream + codecvt

userStories.title: Domain foundation — oBase, oDataContainer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the foundational base classes that all domain entities depend on migrated to src/domain/.
acceptanceCriteria: domain_header.h created with common enums and forward declarations
Stubs created for oEvent.h and other dependents
Domain library compiles with dependency on util only

userStories.title: Simple entities — oControl, oPunch
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the simplest domain entities with fewest external dependencies migrated to src/domain/.
acceptanceCriteria: oControl.h/cpp and oPunch.h/cpp migrated to src/domain/
SpecialPunch enum moved to domain_header.h to avoid circular deps
Win32 string functions replaced with meos_util equivalents
Stubs for oCourse, oClass, oRunner, oCard, oFreePunch
Unit tests for oControl and oPunch

userStories.title: Domain entity — oClub
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the club entity migrated to src/domain/.

userStories.title: Domain entity — oCourse
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the course entity migrated to src/domain/ with full implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: oCourse.h/cpp migrated to src/domain/ (full implementation, not stub)
Length, climb, and course properties preserved

userStories.title: Domain entity — oClass + class configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the class entity and related configuration migrated to src/domain/.
acceptanceCriteria: oClass.h/cpp migrated to src/domain/ (full implementation, not stub)
Start methods (individual, mass, pursuit, etc.) preserved

userStories.title: Domain entities — oCard + oFreePunch
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the card readout and free punch entities migrated to src/domain/.
acceptanceCriteria: oCard.h/cpp migrated to src/domain/ (full implementation, not stub)
SICard.h data structure available in src/util/ or src/domain/

userStories.title: Domain entity — oRunner
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the runner entity migrated to src/domain/ with full implementation. This is the largest and most complex entity (~230KB).
acceptanceCriteria: oRunner.h/cpp migrated to src/domain/ (full implementation replacing stub)
Status computation (OK, DNS, DNF, DSQ, MP) works
oAbstractRunner shared logic with oTeam preserved
Unit tests for runner status, splits, and result computation
userStories.notes: Risk: oRunner is extremely large and coupled. Consider migrating a simplified version first, then backfilling logic incrementally.

userStories.title: Domain entity — oTeam
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the team entity migrated to src/domain/ with full implementation.

userStories.title: Domain aggregate root — oEvent
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the full oEvent implementation migrated as the aggregate root owning all domain collections.
acceptanceCriteria: oEvent.cpp full implementation replaces skeleton stub
oEventDraw.cpp — class drawing and start list generation migrated
Event owns and manages all entity collections
Integration tests covering entity relationships through oEvent
userStories.notes: Risk: oEvent is massive. Migrate core ownership first, then draw, then results.

userStories.title: GeneralResult + result engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the pluggable result computation engine migrated to src/domain/.
acceptanceCriteria: Strategy pattern for scoring algorithms preserved
Result computation produces correct results for individual and relay formats
Unit tests for result computation with known test data

userStories.title: Domain cleanup — remove stubs and verify
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all temporary stubs removed and the domain layer verified to compile cleanly with no Win32 dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: All stubs (gdioutput stub, oSpeaker stub, Table stub, MeOSFeatures stub) removed or replaced with proper interfaces
No #ifdef _WIN32 guards in domain code (except win_types.h shims)
No direct Win32/GDI includes in domain code

userStories.title: Database abstraction + connection + migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want SQLite connection management and a migration system set up in src/db/.
acceptanceCriteria: src/db/ contains SQLiteDatabase with connection management
Initial schema (V1) for runners and clubs
Unit tests for database operations and migrations

userStories.title: Schema for simple entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I want schema and CRUD for clubs, controls, and courses in the SQLite layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema migration for clubs, controls, courses
CRUD operations (insert, get, update, delete) for each entity
Unit tests for each entity's CRUD operations

userStories.title: Schema for complex entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I want schema and CRUD for runners, classes, cards, and punches in the SQLite layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema migration for runners, classes, cards, punches, free punches
CRUD operations with relationship loading (e.g., runner with club/class)
Unit tests for complex entity operations

userStories.title: Schema for events + teams
userStories.description: As a developer, I want event and team persistence completing the full SQLite schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema migration for events and teams
Full integration test: create event -> add entities -> query back
SQLite database is a single file in the working directory

userStories.title: API framework + error handling
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want the HTTP server, routing infrastructure, JSON serialization, and error handling set up.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP server (cpp-httplib or restbed) integrated and running
JSON request parsing and response serialization (nlohmann-json)
Consistent error response format with error codes
Input validation framework with meaningful error messages
Unit tests for routing and error handling

userStories.title: Club + control endpoints
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want CRUD endpoints for clubs and controls.

userStories.title: Course + class endpoints
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want CRUD endpoints for courses and classes including their relationships.

userStories.title: Runner + team endpoints
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want CRUD endpoints for runners and teams with filtering and pagination.

userStories.title: Competition operations API
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want API endpoints for competition-specific operations (start lists, results, card readout).
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/results returns computed results (per class, overall)
POST /api/v1/cards handles card readout data submission
POST /api/v1/runners/{id}/status allows manual status changes (DNS, DNF, DSQ)
Result computation uses existing GeneralResult logic
Endpoints support both preliminary and final results

userStories.title: React + TypeScript web GUI shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want a modern web-based interface shell so that I can manage competitions from any browser.
acceptanceCriteria: React 18+ app with TypeScript in src/ui/web/
Routing with React Router (tabs map to routes: /runners, /classes, /clubs, etc.)
API client layer with typed interfaces matching REST API
Basic layout: navigation sidebar/tabs + content area
Responsive design (works on tablets for field use)
Production build outputs static files that the C++ server serves

userStories.title: Web GUI — competition management
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to create and configure competitions, manage classes, courses, and clubs through the web interface.
acceptanceCriteria: CRUD interface for classes (name, course assignment, start method)
CRUD interface for courses (name, length, controls)
Form validation with user-friendly error messages

userStories.title: Web GUI — runner & team CRUD
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want basic create/read/update/delete for runners and teams in the web interface.
acceptanceCriteria: CRUD interface for runners (name, club, class, start time, card number)
CRUD interface for teams (name, club, class, members)
Search/filter runners by name, club, class
Form validation with user-friendly error messages

userStories.title: Web GUI — import runners
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to import runners from external data sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Preview before import with conflict detection

userStories.title: Web GUI — bulk operations
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want efficient operations on multiple runners at once.

userStories.title: Web GUI — results & start list views
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to view results and start lists in the web interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Results view per class with split times

userStories.title: Web GUI — live results
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want auto-updating results for live competition monitoring.

userStories.title: Web GUI — results export
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to export results in standard formats.

userStories.title: IOF 3.0 XML import/export
userStories.description: As a developer, I want IOF XML import and export migrated to src/io/.
acceptanceCriteria: IOF 3.0 XML import works (runners, classes, clubs, courses)
IOF 3.0 XML export works (results, start lists)
Unit tests with sample IOF XML files

userStories.title: CSV import/export
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CSV data exchange migrated to src/io/.
acceptanceCriteria: CSV import for runners migrated to src/io/
CSV export for results/start lists migrated

userStories.title: HTML result generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want HTML output for results and lists migrated to src/io/.
acceptanceCriteria: HTML result generation migrated to src/io/

userStories.title: PDF generation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want PDF output using libharu migrated to src/io/.
acceptanceCriteria: PDF output works on Linux and Windows

userStories.title: Static file serving from C++ server
userStories.description: As a user, I want the C++ executable to serve the React frontend so that no separate web server is needed.
acceptanceCriteria: C++ HTTP server serves static files from an embedded or bundled directory
index.html served at / with SPA fallback (all non-API routes serve index.html)
React production build is integrated into CMake build process

userStories.title: CI/CD pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated builds, tests, and quality checks on every push and PR.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHub Actions workflow builds on Linux and Windows
All C++ tests run in CI
All frontend tests run in CI
Linting: clang-tidy for C++, ESLint + Prettier for TypeScript
Build artifacts uploaded (executables for Linux and Windows)
CI runs on push to main and on pull requests

userStories.title: Remove Win32 GUI code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Win32-specific GUI code removed so that the codebase is platform-independent.
acceptanceCriteria: All Tab*.cpp/h files removed or migrated to web equivalents
gdioutput.cpp/h and related GDI files removed
Win32-specific APIs (CreateWindow, SendMessage, etc.) eliminated from core code
No Windows-only headers (windows.h, commctrl.h) in domain or server code
Application compiles and runs on Linux without Win32 dependencies
